content.cn_text: 你将听到两个学生，萨姆和莉兹，在讨论他们市场营销课程的一个项目。
content.en_text: You will hear two students, Sam and Liz, discussing a project for their marketing course.

content.cn_text: 山姆:莉兹，我真的很担心我们要一起做的这个营销项目。评估是基于一个单一的，为期一年的项目。
content.en_text: Sam: Liz, I'm getting really worried about this marketing project we have to do together.Assessment is based on a single, year-long project.

content.cn_text: 丽斯:哦!别担心，萨姆。所需要的只是适当的计划。我以前在金融部门工作，我们总是把一年分成几个季度，所以我们就一个季度一个季度地做一个全年计划。
content.en_text: Liz: Oh! don't worry Sam. All it takes is appropriate planning. I used to work in finance, and we always divided the year into quarters, so let's just do a quarter-by-quarter, year-long plan.

content.cn_text: 山姆:嗯，我打算首先采访人们;我是说，这个项目需要原始数据，对吧?
content.en_text: Sam: Well, I intend to firstly interview people; I mean, we need raw data for this project,right?

content.cn_text: 丽兹:好吧，那是你的工作。所以，我们只需要协调我的贡献，这样我们就不会在开始的时候出现双重情况。例如，我可以对所有的理论问题和背景进行研究。
content.en_text: Liz: Okay, that's your job. So, we just need to co-ordinate my contribution, so that we don't double up at the beginning. I can carry out the research on all the theoretical issues and background, for example.

content.cn_text: 山姆:嗯，你为什么不把主要课文的摘要也做一下呢?你知道，把它们写下来。
content.en_text: Sam: Well, why don't you do the summaries of the main texts, as well? You know, write them up.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:哦，我想我们已经对这些课文很熟悉了，而且这只是可选的，所以我们就不用麻烦了。好吧。现在，进入今年第二季度，我将开始从我的书中收集所有相关的信息、细节、事实和例子
content.en_text: Liz: Oh, I think we know those texts well enough already, and it's only optional anyway, so let's not bother with it. Okay. Now, moving onto the second quarter of the year, I'll start collecting all the relevant information, the details, facts, and examples from my book

content.cn_text: 阅读，同时你可以对从面试中获得的第一手信息进行分析。
content.en_text: reading, while you can do an analysis of the first-hand information you got from the interviews.

content.cn_text: 山姆:好的。然后是休息时间。我想说，到那时，年中，你和我都必须决定我们的观点。
content.en_text: Sam: Okay. And then it's break time. I would say, by then, mid-year, both you and I will have to decide on our point of view.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:以及我们如何在文章中支持它，在此基础上，我们可以一起为最终的写作设计一个合适的结构，当我们展示所有东西的时候。
content.en_text: Liz: And how we'll support it in the essay, and based on that, we can work together on designing an appropriate structure for the final writing, when we present everything.

content.cn_text: 山姆:我们当然需要参考书目。我可以在第三季度完成，但既然你做了所有的理论研究，这对你来说是更好的工作。
content.en_text: Sam: We'll need a bibliography of course. I could do that in the third quarter, but since you did all the theoretical research, it would be a better job for you.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:我们可以在年底的时候做，但是我会在我开始写论文的时候列一个初步的清单。同时，您可以开始将数值数据整理成易于阅读的格式。这就是其他学生要做的——你知道，将统计数据用表格形式分组，或者用图形表示。
content.en_text: Liz: We can do that at the very end of the year, but I'll make the preliminary list as I begin writing the essay proper. Meanwhile, you can begin sorting out the numerical data into easily readable formats. That's what the other students will be doing — you know, grouping statistics in tabular form, or making graphical representations.

content.cn_text: 山姆:在最后一个季度，我们可以校对彼此的工作，让不同的人参与进来。所以，我会检查你的写作是否有任何错误、错别字或逻辑问题。我相信这篇文章一定会受益于这样彻底的校对。
content.en_text: Sam: And in the last quarter we can proofread each other's work, allowing a different set of eyes to be involved. So, I'll check your writing for any mistakes, typos, or logical problems. I'm sure the essay will certainly benefit from such a thorough proofread.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:我还会查一下你们所有关于同样问题的统计数据和图表。
content.en_text: Liz: And I'll check all your tabulated statistics and graphs for the same issues.

content.cn_text: 山姆:然后我们把它们放在一起。现在听起来没那么可怕了。
content.en_text: Sam: And then we'll just put it all together. Well, now it sounds far less scary.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:没错。你所需要的只是一个清晰、有逻辑、连贯的计划。
content.en_text: Liz: That's right. All you need is a clear, logical, and coherent plan.

content.cn_text: 山姆:正如我们讨论过的，莉兹，我在这个项目中的第一个任务是通过采访来获取数据——超过100次。要做到这一点，我需要在大学布告栏上贴一张传单，征集志愿者。
content.en_text: Sam: As we discussed, Liz, my first task on this project is to get data using interviews —over 100 of them. To do that, I'll need to put a flyer on the university noticeboard, to get volunteers.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:好吧，照做了，我们马上就可以开始了。
content.en_text: Liz: Okay, just do that, and we can start immediately.

content.cn_text: 山姆:但是我去年就试过了，几乎没有人回复。结果整个计划失败了。
content.en_text: Sam: But I tried this last year, and almost no one replied. The whole project was a failure as a result.

content.cn_text: 莉斯:你只需要确保传单设计得好就行了。我学过设计，所以我可以帮你。
content.en_text: Liz: You just need to ensure that the flyer is designed well. I did a course in design, so I can help you.

content.cn_text: 山姆:我洗耳恭听。
content.en_text: Sam: I'm all ears.

content.cn_text: 莉兹:嗯，由于布告栏会贴满纸，首要规则之一就是要确保你的传单很显眼。因此，不要使用白纸;使用彩色纸。
content.en_text: Liz: Well, one of the first rules, since the noticeboard will be full of papers, is to make sure your flyer stands out. Thus, don't use white paper; use coloured paper.

content.cn_text: 山姆:你是说，像黄色的纸?
content.en_text: Sam: You mean, like yellow paper?

content.cn_text: 莉斯:黄色、红色、橙色，没关系，只要有颜色就行，这样更明显。同样地，让标题吸引人，金钱就能做到这一点，对吧?
content.en_text: Liz: Yellow, red, orange, it doesn't matter, as long as it's coloured, and thus more obvious.Similarly, make the heading attract people, and money does this, right?

content.cn_text: 山姆:当然。每个人都想要钱。
content.en_text: Sam: Sure. Everyone wants money.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:所以，不要说一些无聊的话，比如“需要帮助”，而是说一些“轻松赚钱”。这将立即吸引人们的兴趣。还要确保他们知道这不会花太多时间。这些面试大约半小时，所以告诉他们，就在标题下面。
content.en_text: Liz: So, don't say something boring, such as 'Help needed', but something such as' Easy Money'. That will immediately attract people's interest. And also ensure they know that it won't take much time. These interviews are about half an hour, so tell them that, right under the heading.

content.cn_text: 山姆:听起来不错。继续。
content.en_text: Sam: Sounds good. Keep going.

content.cn_text: 莉兹:嗯，同样的道理，人们也没有时间读书。他们不想在大量的单词中挣扎，因此，限制他们。这将使传单容易和快速阅读。最后，有很多虚假的广告和狡猾的人在那里，所以读者可能会怀疑，特别是当你提供可观的钱为小工作。因此，你需要向他们保证这是合法的，所以，在页面的顶部，确保你表明这个方案已经得到了课程召集人的批准——只是一些书面的说法，但你也应该添加一个签名。谁签署并不重要;反正大家都不读，事实上，你甚至可以自己签名。
content.en_text: Liz: Well, by the same token, people don't have the time to read. They don't want to struggle through a large number of words, thus, limit them. That will make the flyer easily and quickly read. Finally, there's a lot of false advertising and tricky people out there, so the readers might get suspicious, particularly when you're offering significant money for little work. You need to thus assure them that this is legitimate, so, at the very top of the page, make sure you indicate that the scheme has been approved by the course convener—just some writing saying this, but you should also add  a signature. It doesn't matter who signs it; people don't read it anyway, in fact, you could even sign it yourself.

content.cn_text: 山姆:别担心。我可以得到合法的签名。
content.en_text: Sam: Don't worry. I can get a legitimate signature.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:但是你一定要贴上邮票，这样读者会更放心。
content.en_text: Liz: But you should definitely include a stamp, which is more reassuring to the readers.

content.cn_text: 萨姆:什么样的邮票?
content.en_text: Sam: What sort of stamp?

content.cn_text: 丽兹:部门邮票。有些人贴大学的章，但是，说实话，任何人都可以从学生会获得。你们部门的印章更能显示真伪。这样，每个人都会觉得这个计划是合法的，并希望愿意给你打电话。
content.en_text: Liz: The department stamp. Some people put the university stamp, but, to be honest,anyone can acquire that from the student union. A stamp from your department shows much more authenticity. And that way, everyone will feel that the scheme is legitimate,and hopefully be willing to give you a ring.

content.cn_text: 山姆:就这样吗?都做完了?
content.en_text: Sam: So that's it? It's all finished?

content.cn_text: 丽兹:就是这样，当然，除了页面底部的联系方式之外。
content.en_text: Liz: That's it, apart from the contact details at the bottom of the page, of course.

content.cn_text: 山姆:当然可以。嘿，你的建议很有用。我想这样的传单会吸引他们的。谢谢你。
content.en_text: Sam: Of course. Hey, that was some useful advice. I think a flyer like that will really draw them in. Thanks for that.

content.cn_text: 丽兹:没问题。
content.en_text: Liz: No problem.

groups.desc:
Questions 21-24
Complete the plan.
Write the correct letter from the list, A-H, next to the questions.

content: draw conclusions, and design essay structure

items.analysis: 根据对话内容，Liz在第一季度（即项目开始阶段）的任务是进行理论研究。她明确表示她可以"carry out the research on all the theoretical issues and background"，这与选项F "Do theoretical research"直接对应。因此，F是正确答案。
这道题的正确答案是H（Analyse data），因为在原文中，Liz明确指出Sam在第二季度的任务是分析从采访中获得的第一手信息。这与选项H"分析数据"完全吻合。虽然Liz自己会收集信息，但问题特别询问的是Sam在第二季度的任务。
正确答案是E（Produce graphs & tables），因为听力材料中明确提到Sam的任务是将数据整理成易读的格式，包括"tabular form"（表格形式）和"graphical representations"（图形表示）。这直接对应了选项E中的"Produce graphs & tables"（制作图表和表格）。此外，这项任务被安排在第三季度，与题目中的时间安排相符。
正确答案是B. Proofread the essay，因为在对话中Sam明确提到在最后一个季度他们会互相校对对方的工作。Sam说他会检查Liz的写作，寻找任何错误、拼写错误或逻辑问题，这正是校对（proofread）的定义。此外，Sam还强调了这种彻底的校对对论文的重要性，进一步确认了这项任务。

groups.desc:
Questions 25-30
Complete the labels identifying the parts of the 'flyer '.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

items.analysis: 文中 Liz 提到，为了让传单更显眼，不要使用白纸，而是使用彩色纸。因此，题目中要求填写的内容是“Use”后面应该是“coloured paper”。这句话明确指出了答案，学生只需要注意听到“use coloured paper”即可确定答案。
答案是“Easy Money”。题目中的定位词是“heading”，并在原文中由“heading attract people”作为信号词。因此，答案句中提到“don't say something boring, such as 'Help needed', but something such as 'Easy Money'”，表示正确答案应是“Easy Money”。

文中 Liz 说“people don't have the time to read. They don't want to struggle through a large number of words, thus, limit them.”（人们没有时间阅读。他们不想费劲读大量的文字，所以，要限制它们。）这里明确提到要“限制”文字的数量，因此答案是“limit”。题干中的“number of words”是定位词，原文中的“large number of words”是对应的信号词，通过这些信息可以确定答案。
在原文中，Liz 明确指出，Sam 需要确保读者知道该项目得到了课程召集人的批准，并且还需要添加一个签名。因此，"signature" 是正确答案，因为它是确保读者信任的重要部分。题干中的“approval from convener”在听力材料中有同义替换，即“approved by the course convener”，这是学生应注意的信号词。

因为听力原文中明确提到 “you should definitely include a stamp, which is more reassuring to the readers”， 而“a stamp from your department shows much more authenticity”，显然题目问的是在得到召集人的批准时使用什么，那么就是“department stamp”。文中解释了为什么用部门印章比用大学印章更好，所以选“department stamp”是正确的。
因为在听力原文中，Liz 提到“That's it, apart from the contact details at the bottom of the page, of course.”，所以可以确定答案是“contact details”，即联系信息。
